---
title: "The Nobel Prize"
output: 
  flexdashboard::flex_dashboard:
    theme: flatly
    orientation: rows
    vertical_layout: fill
runtime: shiny
---

```{r global, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE
)

library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(highcharter)

Nobel <- read.csv("Nobel_final_data.csv")
```

Introduction
==========================


#### Background Information

The Nobel Prize is one of the most prestigious international awards, recognizing individuals and organizations that have made major contributions to science, literature, economics, and peace. 

In this Shiny dashboard, we analyze Nobel Prize data collected from the official Nobel Prize API. The dataset includes information about laureates such as age, gender, birth country, prize category, prize amount, and year awarded.

Using this project, we explore historical trends in Nobel Prize awards across different time periods and categories. We also investigate patterns related to gender representation and geographical distribution in order to better understand inequalities and changes in recognition over time.



#### Data Sources

- [Nobel Prize Website](https://www.nobelprize.org/)
- [Nobel Prize API Page](https://www.nobelprize.org/about/developer-zone-2/)
- [Nobel Prize API](https://api.nobelprize.org/2.1/laureates)

#### GitHub

Here's a link to our [code](https://github.com/)

Column
-------------------------

```{r, out.width="40%"}
knitr::include_graphics("nobel_medal.webp.png")
```

Overview
==========================

Column
-------------------------

```{r, echo=FALSE}
Nnobel <- Nobel |>
  group_by(Prize_year) |>
  summarise(number_of_laureates = n()) |>
  drop_na(Prize_year)

ggplot(Nnobel, aes(x = Prize_year, y = number_of_laureates)) +
  geom_point( colour = "orange") +
  geom_smooth(se = FALSE, colour = "pink") +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Nobel Laureates Over Time",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal() 
```

Column
-------------------------

```{r, echo=FALSE}
Nnnobel <- Nobel |> 
  mutate(Cent = case_when(
    Prize_year >= 1901 & Prize_year <= 1925 ~ "1901-1925",
    Prize_year >= 1926 & Prize_year <= 1950 ~ "1926-1950",
    Prize_year >= 1951 & Prize_year <= 1975 ~ "1951-1975",
    Prize_year >= 1976 & Prize_year <= 2000 ~ "1976-2000",
    Prize_year >= 2001 & Prize_year <= 2025 ~ "2001-2025"),
  Cent = factor(Cent, levels = c(
      "1901-1925","1926-1950","1951-1975","1976-2000","2001-2025")),
  Category = factor(Category)) |>
  drop_na(Category, Cent) |>
  count(Category, Cent, .drop = FALSE) 

highchart() |>
  hc_add_series(data = Nnnobel,
                type = "column", 
                hcaes(x = Cent ,
                      y = n, 
                      group = Category)) |>
  hc_plotOptions(
    column = list(stacking = "percent")  
  ) |>
  hc_xAxis(
    categories = unique(Nnnobel$Cent),
    title = list(text = "Cent")
  ) |>
  hc_yAxis(title = list(text = "Number of Prizes"))
```

Column
-------------------------

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

Nobbel <- Nobel |>
  drop_na(Age)

ggplot(data = Nobbel, aes(x = Age)) +
  geom_histogram() +
  labs(title = "Laureate Age Distribution", x = "Age", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()
```

Gender analysis
==========================

Column
-------------------------

```{r, echo=FALSE}
library(dplyr)

Nobel_3 <- Nobel |> 
  mutate(Cent = case_when(
    Prize_year >= 1901 & Prize_year <= 1925 ~ "1901-1925",
    Prize_year >= 1926 & Prize_year <= 1950 ~ "1926-1950",
    Prize_year >= 1951 & Prize_year <= 1975 ~ "1951-1975",
    Prize_year >= 1976 & Prize_year <= 2000 ~ "1976-2000",
    Prize_year >= 2001 & Prize_year <= 2025 ~ "2001-2025"
  )) |>
  drop_na(Gender) |>
  count(Cent, Gender)

highchart() |>
  hc_add_series(data = Nobel_3,
                type = "column", 
                hcaes(x = Cent,
                      y = n,
                      group = Gender)) |>
  hc_xAxis(categories = unique(Nobel_3$Cent),
    title = list(text = "Year")) |>
  hc_yAxis(title = list(text="Number of Laureates")) |>
  hc_plotOptions(series = list(animation = TRUE)) |>
  hc_legend(align = "right", 
            verticalAlign = "top") |>
  hc_colors(c("#F8BBD0", "#F48FB1", "#F06292", "#EC407A", "#D81B60"))
```


Column
-------------------------

```{r, echo=FALSE}
Nobel_6 <- Nobel |>
  filter(!is.na(Gender)) |>
  count(Gender) -> Gender

highchart() |> 
  hc_chart(type ="column",
           options3d = list(enabled = TRUE, beta = 15, alpha = 15)) |>
  hc_xAxis(categories = unique(Nobel_6$Gender),
    title = list(text = "Gender")) |>
  hc_add_series(data = Gender$n, name = "Gender") |>
  hc_colors(c("#F8BBD0", "#F48FB1", "#F06292", "#EC407A", "#D81B60"))
```


Column
-------------------------

```{r}
ggplot(Nobel, aes(x = Gender, y = Age)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Laureate Age by Gender", x = "Gender", y = "Age") +
  theme_minimal()
```

Column
-------------------------

```{r}
Nobeel <- Nobel |>
  drop_na(Gender, Amount) 

ggplot(Nobeel, aes(x = Gender, y = Amount)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Nobel Prize Amount by Gender", x = "Gender", y = "Prize Amount") +
  theme_minimal()
```

Maps
==========================

Column
-------------------------

```{r}
library(leaflet)
library(rnaturalearth)
library(rnaturalearthdata)
library(dplyr)
library(sf)
library(tidyr)

# creating a country_count data set with names matching earth data
nobel_count <- Nobel |>
  filter(!is.na(Birth_country)) |>
  mutate(Birth_country = recode(Birth_country,
    "USA"                               = "United States of America",
    "the Netherlands"                   = "Netherlands",
    "South Korea"                       = "South Korea",
    "Korea"                             = "South Korea",
    "USSR"                              = "Russia",
    "Russian Empire"                    = "Russia",
    "Prussia"                           = "Germany",
    "Bavaria"                           = "Germany",
    "Mecklenburg"                       = "Germany",
    "Württemberg"                       = "Germany",
    "Hesse-Kassel"                      = "Germany",
    "West Germany"                      = "Germany",
    "East Friesland"                    = "Germany",
    "Schleswig"                         = "Germany",
    "Free City of Danzig"               = "Poland",
    "German-occupied Poland"            = "Poland",
    "Austria-Hungary"                   = "Austria",
    "Austrian Empire"                   = "Austria",
    "Ottoman Empire"                    = "Turkey",
    "Persia"                            = "Iran",
    "British Protectorate of Palestine" = "Israel",
    "British Mandate of Palestine"      = "Israel",
    "British India"                     = "India",
    "Burma"                             = "Myanmar",
    "Tuscany"                           = "Italy",
    "Gold Coast"                        = "Ghana",
    "Belgian Congo"                     = "Dem. Rep. Congo",
    "Southern Rhodesia"                 = "Zimbabwe",
    "French Algeria"                    = "Algeria",
    "French protectorate of Tunisia"    = "Tunisia",
    "Czechoslovakia"                    = "Czechia",
    "East Timor"                        = "Timor-Leste",
    "Java, Dutch East Indies"           = "Indonesia",
    "Faroe Islands (Denmark)"           = "Denmark",
    "Guadeloupe, France"                = "France",
    "British West Indies"               = "Trinidad and Tobago",
    "Crete"                             = "Greece",
    "Scotland"                          = "United Kingdom",
    "Northern Ireland"                  = "United Kingdom",
    "Tibet"                             = "China",
    "Bosnia"                            = "Bosnia and Herz."
  )) |>
  group_by(Birth_country) |>
  summarise(total = n()) # getting the counts for each country as total

# creating a data set with country coordinates and boundaries 
world <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf")

# Merging our country_count data set with the coordinate data set
world_nobel <- world |>
  left_join(nobel_count, by = c("name" = "Birth_country")) |>
  mutate(total = replace_na(total, 0)) #replacing nas with 0 

# selecting a color palette and assigning bins 
pal <- colorBin(
  palette  = "YlOrRd",
  domain   = world_nobel$total[world_nobel$total > 0],
  bins     = c(1, 5, 10, 25, 50, 100, 300),
  na.color = "#f0f0f0"
)

# Map 
leaflet(world_nobel) |>
  addProviderTiles(providers$CartoDB.Positron) |>
  setView(lng = 0, lat = 20, zoom = 2) |>
  addPolygons(
    fillColor    = ~ifelse(total == 0, "#f0f0f0", pal(ifelse(total == 0, NA, total))),
    fillOpacity  = ~ifelse(total == 0, 0.3, 0.8),
    color        = "white",
    weight       = 0.5,
    label        = ~paste0(name, ": ", ifelse(total == 0, "None", paste0(total, " Nobel Prize(s)"))),# saying none for countries without prizes
    highlight    = highlightOptions(
      weight       = 3,
      color        = "#333",
      bringToFront = TRUE
    )
  ) |>
  addLegend(
    pal      = pal,
    values   = world_nobel$total[world_nobel$total > 0],
    title    = "Nobel Prizes",
    position = "bottomright",
    labFormat = labelFormat(suffix = " prizes")
  )
```

Column
-------------------------

```{r, echo = FALSE}
library(dplyr)

Nobell <- Nobel |>
  filter(!is.na(Birth_country)) |>
  count(Birth_country, sort = TRUE) |>
  slice_max(n, n = 10)

highchart() |>
  hc_add_series(data = Nobell,
                type = "column", 
                hcaes(x = Birth_country,
                      y = n)) |>
  hc_xAxis(categories = unique(Nobell$Birth_country),
    title = list(text = "Country")) |>
  hc_yAxis(title = list(text="Number of Prizes")) |>
  hc_colors(c("#F8BBD0", "#F48FB1", "#F06292", "#EC407A", "#D81B60"))

```


Column
-------------------------

```{r}
Noobel <- Nobel |>
  drop_na(Age, Amount)

highchart() |>
  hc_add_series(
    data = Noobel,
    type = "scatter",
    hcaes(x = Age, y = Amount)
  ) |>
  hc_xAxis(title = list(text = "Age")) |>
  hc_yAxis(title = list(text = "Prize Amount")) |>
  hc_colors(c("#F8BBD0", "#F48FB1", "#F06292", "#EC407A", "#D81B60"))
```
